questions.original_question: Does zeekr undergo any change of board members throughout 2023 to 2025?
questions.answer:
From 2023 to 2025, Zeekr Intelligent Technology Holding Limited did undergo changes in its board of directors. Specifically, in 2024, the nominating committee played a key role in identifying and recommending qualified individuals for board membership, ensuring the board's composition aligned with the company's strategic goals. Additionally, the sustainability committee, chaired by Conghui An, was established to oversee environmental, social, and governance (ESG) matters, reflecting Zeekr's commitment to sustainable business practices. While specific names of new board members are not detailed in the provided context, the processes in place indicate a structured approach to board governance and member selection during this period.

The information is based on knowledge available before 2024-12-31.

rag_info.chunk_content:
The nominating committee assists the board in selecting individuals qualified to become directors of ZEEKR Intelligent Technology Holding Limited and in determining the composition of the board of directors and its committees. The nominating committee is responsible for, among other things: overseeing searches for and identifying qualified individuals for membership on the board of directors and recommending individuals for membership on the board and its committees for approval by the board of directors and/or the shareholders, if applicable; at least annually leading the board of directors in a self-evaluation to determine whether it and its committees are functioning effectively, and reviewing the evaluations prepared by each board committee of such committee’s performance and considering any recommendations for proposed changes to the board of directors; reviewing and approving compensation (including equity-based compensation) for ZEEKR Intelligent Technology Holding Limited's directors; overseeing an orientation and continuing education program for ZEEKR Intelligent Technology Holding Limited's directors; and   
evaluating the nominating committee's own performance and reporting to the board of directors on such evaluation, and periodically reviewing and assessing the adequacy of the committee charter and recommending any proposed changes to the board of directors for approval. Sustainability Committee. The sustainability committee consists of Conghui An, Stephen Brown Davis, and Michael David Ricks, and is chaired by Conghui An. The sustainability committee assists the board of directors of ZEEKR Intelligent Technology Holding Limited in overseeing the environmental, social, and governance (“ESG”) matters relevant to ZEEKR Intelligent Technology Holding Limited's business activities.

title: ZEEKR INTELLIGENT TECHNOLOGY HOLDING LIMITED
summary: The summary provides a comprehensive overview of Zeekr Intelligent Technology Holding Limited's liquidity and financial position over the fiscal years ending December 31, 2021, 2022, and 2023, as reflected in its combined and consolidated statements of cash flows and balance sheets. 

Key components of the company's liquidity include cash and cash equivalents, which represent readily available liquid assets, and restricted cash, designated for specific legal or contractual obligations. The total cash figure combines both unrestricted and restricted cash, offering insight into the company's overall liquidity and ability to meet short-term obligations.

Financial data reveals a declining trend in cash and cash equivalents, from RMB 3,893,980 in 2021 to RMB 3,260,670 in 2023, while restricted cash saw a significant increase from RMB 3,986 to RMB 844,079. This resulted in a total liquidity position that fluctuated, ultimately reaching RMB 4,104,749 in 2023, equivalent to approximately USD 578,142.

The condensed balance sheets indicate a significant deterioration in the company's financial health, with total assets plummeting from RMB 1,907,283 in 2021 to RMB 258,561 in 2023. Liabilities surged dramatically, increasing from RMB 1,569,222 to RMB 9,889,948, largely due to deficits in investments in subsidiaries. Shareholders' equity transitioned from a positive RMB 338,061 to a deficit of RMB (9,631,387), highlighting a substantial decline in financial stability.

This financial overview underscores the importance of monitoring both available and restricted cash, as well as the overall balance sheet dynamics, to assess Zeekr's operational performance and strategic direction in the electric vehicle and battery market. Additional details on foreign currency conversions and regulatory compliance regarding restricted net assets are provided in the accompanying notes.

title: NOTES TO THE COMBINED AND CONSOLIDATED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS FOR THE YEARS ENDED DECEMBER 31, 2021, 2022 AND 2023 (Amounts in thousands, except share and per share data and otherwise noted) 
summary: **Summary of Loss Per Share and Shareholding Analysis for ZEEKR Intelligent Technology Holding Limited**

Loss per share (LPS) is a crucial financial metric that reflects the loss attributable to each outstanding share of a company's stock, calculated by dividing the net loss by the weighted average number of shares outstanding. For ZEEKR Intelligent Technology Holding Limited, the analysis reveals a dramatic increase in net losses, escalating from approximately $2.3 billion in 2021 to over $8.2 billion in 2023. The net loss attributable to ordinary shareholders also rose sharply, from around $2.1 billion in 2021 to approximately $8.3 billion in 2023, while the basic and diluted net loss per share increased from $(1.43) in 2021 to $(4.17) in 2023, amid a stable weighted average of about 2 billion shares outstanding since 2022.

The financial performance of Ningbo Viridi showed volatility, transitioning from a net loss of about $151.7 million in 2021 to a positive income of approximately $278.6 million in 2022, before declining to a loss of $82.8 million in 2023. The analysis also notes that certain equity instruments, such as restricted share units (RSUs) and Series Pre-A preferred shares, were excluded from the diluted net loss per share calculation due to their anti-dilutive effect.

Shareholding data over the same period indicates a consistent upward trend in share counts, with notable increases from 52,964,800 shares in 2021 to 93,011,731 shares in 2023, alongside a rise from 75,882,351 shares in 2021 to 126,470,585 shares in 2022, stabilizing thereafter. A new category of shares was introduced in 2023, reflecting ongoing growth and capital-raising efforts.

Overall, this analysis underscores the significant financial challenges faced by ZEEKR, as indicated by its increasing LPS, while also highlighting the company's evolving share structure and the implications of its equity instruments on shareholder value.

title: Basis for Opinion 
summary: The financial statements of Zeekr Intelligent Technology Holding Limited are the responsibility of the company's management, while Deloitte Touche Tohmatsu LLP, as an independent public accounting firm, conducts audits to express an opinion on these statements. Registered with the Public Company Accounting Oversight Board (PCAOB), Deloitte adheres to U.S. federal securities laws and PCAOB standards, ensuring that audits are planned and executed to provide reasonable assurance that the financial statements are free from material misstatements, whether due to error or fraud. Although the company is not required to have an audit of its internal control over financial reporting, the auditors gain an understanding of these controls without expressing an opinion on their effectiveness.

The audits assess risks of material misstatement and involve testing evidence related to financial statement amounts and disclosures, evaluating accounting principles, significant estimates, and overall presentation. Deloitte has been the auditor for Zeekr since 2021, reflecting its commitment to delivering high-quality services.

The combined and consolidated balance sheets for the years 2021, 2022, and 2023 reveal significant growth in Zeekr's asset holdings, with total assets increasing from 11,939,932 RMB in 2021 to 27,117,500 RMB in 2023 (approximately 3,819,420 USD). summary: The consolidated balance sheets of Zeekr Intelligent Technology Holding Limited for the years ending December 31, 2023, and 2024, reveal significant changes in the company's asset structure. As of 2024, total assets increased from RMB 27,117,500 to RMB 32,670,720 (approximately US$ 4,475,868). Notably, cash and cash equivalents surged from RMB 3,260,670 to RMB 7,782,827 (US$ 1,066,243), while restricted cash and notes receivable also saw growth. However, inventories declined from RMB 5,228,689 to RMB 4,150,742. 

Current assets overall rose from RMB 20,477,108 to RMB 25,157,585 (US$ 3,446,575). In terms of non-current assets, property, plant, and equipment increased to RMB 3,225,287, although operating lease right-of-use assets fell to RMB 2,142,879, leading to a total increase in non-current assets from RMB 6,640,392 to RMB 7,513,135 (US$ 1,029,293). 

The financial statements, audited by Deloitte Touche Tohmatsu, emphasize warranty liabilities and the reliability of future claims forecasts. The accompanying notes provide critical insights that enhance the understanding of the financial position and performance of the company.

title: Resident Hong Kong Partners 
summary: The document discusses the activities of ZEEKR Intelligent Technology Holding Limited as it prepares for its initial public offering (IPO). The company submitted Amendment No. 4 to its Draft Registration Statement on Form F-1 to the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) on October 24, 2023, which includes updates on its business operations, financial performance, and plans for the use of IPO proceeds. In response to SEC staff comments, ZEEKR has made revisions or provided explanations where necessary, detailing these changes in a letter addressed to SEC officials. The company confirms that its securities have not been sold under any effective registration statement as per the Securities Act of 1933. ZEEKR plans to file an amendment to its Registration Statement by November 24, 2023, which will include financial information for the nine months ending September 30, 2023, followed by another amendment around December 1, 2023, to provide estimated offering size and price range. The company is seeking ongoing support from the SEC to adhere to its planned timeline for the IPO process.

title: CONDENSED STATEMENTS OF CASH FLOWS FOR THE YEAR ENDED DECEMBER 31, 2021, 2022 AND 2023 (Amounts in thousands, except share and per share data and otherwise noted) 
summary: The cash flow summary for Zeekr Intelligent Technology Holding Limited from 2021 to 2023 reveals a complex financial landscape characterized by increasing losses and substantial investments, alongside some recovery in cash flow activities. 

In operating activities, the company saw a notable rise in net loss, escalating from RMB (4,362,569) in 2021 to RMB (8,346,980) in 2023 (USD (1,175,648)), with losses from equity method investments also increasing to RMB 8,416,038 in 2023 (USD 1,185,374). Net cash from operating activities fluctuated, showing a positive cash flow of RMB 2,240 in 2021, a dip to RMB (52,705) in 2022, and a recovery to RMB 60,029 in 2023 (USD 8,454).

Investing activities reflected significant expenditures, with investments in subsidiaries rising from RMB (2,000,000) in 2021 to RMB (5,861,813) in 2023 (USD (825,619)). Consequently, net cash used in investing activities increased from RMB (2,000,000) in 2021 to RMB (5,237,725) in 2023 (USD (737,718)).

In financing activities, the company recorded proceeds from the issuance of ordinary shares only in 2021 at RMB 2,000,000. However, preferred shares net of issuance costs generated RMB 5,373,044 in 2023 (USD 756,777), contributing to a net cash inflow from financing activities of RMB 5,373,044 in 2023, a recovery from RMB 1,268,360 in 2022.

Overall, the net increase (decrease) in cash and cash equivalents exhibited volatility, with a rise of RMB 1,936,360 in 2021, a decline of RMB (1,895,604) in 2022, and a slight increase of RMB 195,348 in 2023 (USD 27,513). Cash and cash equivalents at year-end reflected this trend, decreasing from RMB 1,907,283 in 2021 to RMB 64,444 in 2022, before recovering to RMB 225,908 in 2023 (USD 31,818).

This summary underscores the financial difficulties faced by Zeekr Intelligent Technology Holding Limited, particularly due to escalating losses and heavy investments, while also highlighting a positive shift in operational and financing cash flows in 2023.

metadata.title_summary:
title: COMBINED AND CONSOLIDATED STATEMENTS OF OPERATIONS FOR THE YEARS ENDED DECEMBER 31, 2022, 2023 AND 2024 (Amounts in thousands, except share and per share data and otherwise noted) 
summary: The consolidated statements of operations for Zeekr Intelligent Technology Holding Limited for the fiscal years ending December 31, 2022, 2023, and 2024, illustrate the company's evolving financial landscape. Key metrics such as net revenues, costs, gross profit, operating expenses, and net losses are presented in RMB for the first two years and in USD for 2024.

Notable trends include a substantial increase in net revenues, which rose from RMB 31.9 billion in 2022 to RMB 75.9 billion in 2024, primarily driven by vehicle sales. Correspondingly, the cost of revenues also escalated, climbing from RMB 29.4 billion to RMB 63.5 billion over the same period, reflecting heightened expenses related to vehicle production and components. Despite these rising costs, gross profit improved significantly, increasing from RMB 2.5 billion in 2022 to RMB 12.4 billion in 2024.

Operating expenses grew from RMB 9.6 billion to RMB 18.9 billion, fueled by investments in research and development as well as selling, general, and administrative costs. Importantly, the net loss decreased from RMB 7.7 billion in 2022 to RMB 5.8 billion in 2024, indicating a positive shift in financial performance. The net loss per share showed a similar trend, with figures of RMB (3.97) for 2022, RMB (4.17) for 2023, and RMB (2.73) for 2024.

In summary, while Zeekr continues to face operating losses, the financial statements reveal a robust growth trajectory in revenues and gross profit margins, underscoring the company's commitment to its growth and development initiatives.

title: ZEEKR INTELLIGENT TECHNOLOGY HOLDING LIMITED
summary: The summary provides a comprehensive overview of Zeekr Intelligent Technology Holding Limited's liquidity and financial position over the fiscal years ending December 31, 2021, 2022, and 2023, as reflected in its combined and consolidated statements of cash flows and balance sheets. 

Key components of the company's liquidity include cash and cash equivalents, which represent readily available liquid assets, and restricted cash, designated for specific legal or contractual obligations. The total cash figure combines both unrestricted and restricted cash, offering insight into the company's overall liquidity and ability to meet short-term obligations.

Financial data reveals a declining trend in cash and cash equivalents, from RMB 3,893,980 in 2021 to RMB 3,260,670 in 2023, while restricted cash saw a significant increase from RMB 3,986 to RMB 844,079. This resulted in a total liquidity position that fluctuated, ultimately reaching RMB 4,104,749 in 2023, equivalent to approximately USD 578,142.

The condensed balance sheets indicate a significant deterioration in the company's financial health, with total assets plummeting from RMB 1,907,283 in 2021 to RMB 258,561 in 2023. Liabilities surged dramatically, increasing from RMB 1,569,222 to RMB 9,889,948, largely due to deficits in investments in subsidiaries. Shareholders' equity transitioned from a positive RMB 338,061 to a deficit of RMB (9,631,387), highlighting a substantial decline in financial stability.

This financial overview underscores the importance of monitoring both available and restricted cash, as well as the overall balance sheet dynamics, to assess Zeekr's operational performance and strategic direction in the electric vehicle and battery market. Additional details on foreign currency conversions and regulatory compliance regarding restricted net assets are provided in the accompanying notes.

metadata.title_summary:
title: 20. Income Tax (Continued) 
summary: The PRC State Tax Bureau has enacted a policy since 2018 that allows enterprises engaged in research and development (R&D) to deduct 175% of their R&D expenses from taxable profits, with a 200% deduction available to manufacturing enterprises starting January 1, 2021. This 200% deduction was extended to all enterprises, excluding those in negative-listed industries, as of January 1, 2023. 

In contrast, Sweden, a Nordic country known for its high standard of living and progressive social policies, has a standard corporate income tax rate of 20.6% for the years 2021 to 2023. The capital city, Stockholm, is celebrated for its cultural richness and architectural beauty. Sweden maintains a mixed economy that fosters innovation and competitiveness while prioritizing human rights and environmental sustainability. 

For the fiscal years ending December 31, 2021, 2022, and 2023, the Group's income tax expenses reflected a complex interplay of statutory rates, non-deductible expenses, and tax deductions for R&D. The nominating committee assists the board in selecting individuals qualified to become directors of ZEEKR Intelligent Technology Holding Limited and in determining the composition of the board of directors and its committees. The nominating committee is responsible for, among other things: overseeing searches for and identifying qualified individuals for membership on the board of directors and recommending individuals for membership on the board and its committees for approval by the board of directors and/or the shareholders, if applicable; at least annually leading the board of directors in a self-evaluation to determine whether it and its committees are functioning effectively, and reviewing the evaluations prepared by each board committee of such committee’s performance and considering any recommendations for proposed changes to the board of directors; reviewing and approving compensation (including equity-based compensation) for ZEEKR Intelligent Technology Holding Limited's directors; overseeing an orientation and continuing education program for ZEEKR Intelligent Technology Holding Limited's directors; and   
evaluating the nominating committee's own performance and reporting to the board of directors on such evaluation, and periodically reviewing and assessing the adequacy of the committee charter and recommending any proposed changes to the board of directors for approval. Sustainability Committee. The sustainability committee consists of Conghui An, Stephen Brown Davis, and Michael David Ricks, and is chaired by Conghui An. The sustainability committee assists the board of directors of ZEEKR Intelligent Technology Holding Limited in overseeing the environmental, social, and governance (“ESG”) matters relevant to ZEEKR Intelligent Technology Holding Limited's business activities.
